External teaching sample

Brief Studio

Create, review, and finalize briefs while the app keeps its own GraphQL schema, draft workflow, and orchestration policy over one hosted backend mount.

Waiting for the host bootstrap payload…
Advanced app details

The generic host only launches the application shell and prepares the backend. This app then creates business-owned briefId records, generates drafts later through GraphQL, and reconciles runtime ownership with durable bindingIntentId handoff.

Application
Waiting for bootstrap…
—
Launch
—
—
Backend mount
—
—

Create a brief record

Start with the business record. Runtime resource, model, workspace, and tool-execution defaults now come from the host launch setup instead of this brief canvas.

GraphQL stays the primary brief API; configure runtime and workspace defaults from the host-managed launch setup surface.

Briefs

Track each brief record, its latest draft state, and its review outcome.

No briefs yet. Create a brief, then generate a draft when it is ready for review.

Brief detail

Review generated drafts, notes, and approval state on the same brief record.

Select a brief to review generated drafts, notes, and approval status.

Backend notifications

Optional app notifications fan out through the platform-owned application backend stream.

No notifications yet.
